title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
summary: Ziv Ilan's team at NVIDIA achieved near real-time video diffusion generation on Blackwell B200 by combining dynamic quantization, latent chunk caching, and step distillation to reduce the typical 50-step denoising process to as few as one step. The distillation methods include trajectory-based and distribution-based training approaches, with distribution-based training being more common and higher quality.
writeup: Ziv Ilan of NVIDIA presents how his team reached near real-time video diffusion generation on the Blackwell B200 by stacking three optimizations: dynamic quantization, latent chunk caching, and step distillation. The headline result is collapsing the standard 50-step denoising process down to as few as one step, which matters for the latency and cost of video generation. The talk distinguishes two distillation families, trajectory-based and distribution-based training, and notes that distribution-based training is more common and produces higher quality.
